result.summary: ShipStation exposes two authenticated REST APIs—a current v2 API and a legacy v1 API—plus a public documentation MCP server at `https://docs.shipstation.com/mcp` for AI assistants.
result.description: ShipStation is a shipping and fulfillment platform for ecommerce sellers and businesses. It helps users manage orders, compare rates, buy labels, track shipments, and coordinate fulfillment workflows across carriers and sales channels.

shipstation_v2_api_key.label: ShipStation API v2 companion API key
shipstation_v2_api_key.setup: In your ShipStation account, open the API settings page referenced from the [Security & Authentication guide](https://docs.shipstation.com/authentication) and generate a **v2 Companion API Key**. The guide says you can generate and copy API keys from your account settings, and that only one v2 key can be active at a time. Save the key when generated; the docs say it should be stored securely and can be revoked from account API settings.

shipstation_v1_basic.label: ShipStation API v1 key and secret
shipstation_v1_basic.setup: Sign in to ShipStation and go to [API Settings](https://ss.shipstation.com/#/settings/api). Generate or view your ShipStation `API Key` and `API Secret` for the legacy v1 API. The [V1 requirements page](https://www.shipstation.com/docs/api/requirements/) states these are used as the Basic Auth username and password.
